title: quercus lusitanica f. subinclusa
intro: Quercus lusitanica f. subinclusa is a variant of the Portuguese oak, known as a specific form or cultivar of the Quercus lusitanica tree, which is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and polyphenols. The health benefits of Quercus lusitanica f. subinclusa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as teas, infusions, or capsules, and are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
